summary: Nigeria does not operate a comprehensive digital population register. While the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) maintains a National Identity Database linked to the NIN (National Identification Number), this functions primarily as an identity verification system rather than a population register with comprehensive demographic, address, and family relationship data. The National Population Commission (NPC) conducts periodic censuses but does not maintain a continuously updated digital population register.
notes: Nigeria's last completed census was in 2006, though a new census has been planned. The NIN database, while growing, does not constitute a population register in the traditional sense as it lacks continuous address updates, family linkages, and real-time vital event integration. There is no citizen-facing portal for viewing or updating population register records.
